articles.headline: India and Canada Agree to Restore Normal Diplomatic Relations Post-Dispute
articles.explainer:
• India and Canada have announced a significant agreement to normalize diplomatic ties, marking a de-escalation of tensions that began in 2023.
• The dispute originated from Canadian allegations regarding India's potential involvement in the killing of Sikh separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar in Vancouver.
• The agreement is expected to facilitate the return of high commissioners and other diplomatic staff, along with a resumption of full bilateral engagements.
• This move signals a mutual desire to rebuild trust and cooperation on various fronts, including trade, security, and people-to-people exchanges.
• Analysts suggest the resolution reflects diplomatic efforts from both sides, possibly influenced by pressure from international partners to stabilize relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Dispute originated in 2023 over Hardeep Singh Nijjar's killing.
Nijjar was a prominent leader in the Khalistan movement.
Agreement aims to restore 'normal diplomatic relations'.
mainsPoints: Impact of the dispute on India's global image and diaspora relations.
Geopolitical implications of renewed cooperation between India and Canada.
Challenges and opportunities in rebuilding trust and expanding bilateral trade.
Role of external actors/allies in mediating or influencing the resolution.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Canada side-by-side, symbolizing renewed diplomatic ties.
articles.trivia: Canada is home to one of the largest Sikh diasporas outside of Punjab, India, which often influences bilateral dynamics.

articles.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Watch
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision comes as the MPC continues to prioritize inflation control while supporting economic growth.
• The central bank noted persistent global uncertainties and domestic food inflation as key factors influencing its cautious stance.
• Liquidity conditions in the banking system were also reviewed, with the RBI reiterating its commitment to calibrate policy as needed.
• Experts anticipate that the RBI will maintain a vigilant approach, with any rate cuts contingent on sustained moderation in inflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC consists of 6 members (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by Government).
Primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in managing domestic and global economic headwinds.
Examine the potential impact of a prolonged status quo on interest rates on various sectors of the Indian economy.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was constituted under Section 45ZB of the amended RBI Act, 1934, in 2016.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Declares Right to Clean Air as Fundamental Right Under Article 21
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has delivered a landmark judgment, affirming that the 'Right to Clean Air' is an integral part of the fundamental 'Right to Life' guaranteed by Article 21 of the Constitution.
• This ruling comes in response to a petition highlighting severe air pollution levels across various Indian cities, impacting public health.
• The Court emphasized the state's obligation to protect and improve the environment, ensuring citizens' right to live with dignity.
• The judgment is expected to bolster environmental protection laws and push for more stringent measures against pollution.
• It calls upon central and state governments to formulate and implement comprehensive strategies to combat air pollution effectively.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Right to Life is enshrined in Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
The judgment links environmental quality directly to fundamental rights.
Various environmental rights have been read into Article 21 by the SC over time (e.g., clean water, healthy environment).
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of this judgment for environmental governance and policy-making in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the 'Right to Clean Air' across diverse regions and industrial landscapes.
Examine the role of judicial intervention in strengthening environmental protection and public health in India.
Suggest a multi-pronged strategy for governments to effectively uphold this fundamental right.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and an Indian Constitution book, symbolizing a landmark legal decision.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to a Healthy Environment' is recognized in many international human rights instruments.

articles.headline: ISRO Unveils Ambitious Plans for 'Chandrayaan-4' Lunar Polar Exploration Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has announced its next major lunar mission, 'Chandrayaan-4', focusing on advanced polar exploration.
• The mission aims to conduct in-depth studies of the Moon's polar regions, particularly searching for water ice and understanding lunar geology.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a more complex mission, potentially involving a larger lander, rover, and even a sample return capability.
• This mission builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-1 and Chandrayaan-3, further solidifying India's position in lunar science.
• International collaborations are also being explored for components or scientific instruments, enhancing global space partnerships.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-1 confirmed presence of water molecules on Moon.
Chandrayaan-3 successfully soft-landed near the lunar south pole.
Chandrayaan-4 aims for more advanced studies, potentially including sample return.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar polar regions for future space exploration and potential resource utilization.
Analyze how ISRO's lunar missions contribute to India's scientific capabilities and global standing in space technology.
Examine the economic and strategic benefits of investing in advanced space missions like Chandrayaan-4.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of Chandrayaan-4 spacecraft orbiting the Moon, focusing on the polar region.
articles.trivia: The Moon's polar regions are of particular interest due to permanently shadowed craters believed to harbor significant amounts of water ice.

articles.headline: Government Launches PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana 5.0, Focus on Future-Ready Skills
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship has rolled out the fifth phase of the Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY 5.0).
• This iteration places a strong emphasis on training youth in emerging technologies and industry-relevant skills like AI, IoT, data science, and sustainable practices.
• PMKVY 5.0 aims to align skill development with the demands of the rapidly evolving job market and promote entrepreneurship.
• The scheme incorporates enhanced digital learning modules, industry apprenticeships, and outcome-based assessments.
• Special provisions are included to ensure inclusive participation from marginalized communities and rural areas, bridging the skill gap across India.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Inclusive Growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: PMKVY is a flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
It aims to provide skill training to Indian youth.
PMKVY 5.0 focuses on 'future skills' and 'demand-driven' training.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the success and limitations of previous PMKVY phases in addressing India's skill gap.
Discuss how PMKVY 5.0's focus on emerging technologies can contribute to India's economic growth and global competitiveness.
Suggest measures to ensure that skill development programs are inclusive, industry-relevant, and lead to sustainable employment opportunities.
articles.imageDescription: Young people engaged in various vocational training activities, symbolizing skill development.
articles.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, making skill development crucial for harnessing its demographic dividend.

articles.headline: New National Tiger Conservation Plan Prioritizes Corridor Protection and Community Involvement
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has unveiled an updated National Tiger Conservation Plan (NTCP).
• The revised plan places a strong emphasis on securing and restoring critical tiger corridors to ensure genetic exchange and prevent habitat fragmentation.
• It integrates local communities as key stakeholders in conservation efforts, promoting sustainable livelihoods and reducing human-wildlife conflict.
• Advanced surveillance technologies, including drones and AI-powered monitoring, will be deployed for more effective anti-poaching measures.
• The plan also aims to strengthen transboundary cooperation with neighboring countries for better management of shared tiger habitats.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Environmental governance
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
NTCA (National Tiger Conservation Authority) is a statutory body.
Tiger corridors are crucial for genetic flow between fragmented tiger populations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in tiger conservation in India and how the new NTCP addresses them.
Analyze the importance of community participation and transboundary cooperation in successful wildlife conservation.
Examine the role of technology in anti-poaching and habitat monitoring efforts within the new plan.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage, symbolizing conservation efforts.
articles.trivia: India is home to approximately 70% of the world's wild tiger population.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Global South Concerns at Quad Leaders' Summit
articles.explainer:
• India played a pivotal role in advocating for the interests and concerns of the Global South at the recently concluded Quad Leaders' Summit.
• Prime Minister highlighted issues such as climate justice, equitable access to technology, and sustainable development for developing nations.
• The Quad leaders discussed regional security, maritime cooperation, critical and emerging technologies, and infrastructure development.
• While maintaining its focus on a free and open Indo-Pacific, India emphasized the need for inclusive growth and addressing global challenges with a broader perspective.
• The summit reaffirmed the commitment of Quad nations (India, USA, Japan, Australia) to a rules-based international order and regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Quad members are India, USA, Japan, and Australia.
Quad focuses on a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
India often acts as a voice for the Global South in international forums.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic rationale for engaging with the Quad while simultaneously championing the Global South.
Discuss the effectiveness of the Quad in addressing both traditional security concerns and contemporary challenges like climate change and technology access.
Examine the potential benefits and drawbacks of India's multi-alignment foreign policy approach in the context of the Quad.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders of the Quad nations shaking hands, with flags in the background.
articles.trivia: The Quad was initially conceived in 2007 but gained significant momentum and formalization in the late 2010s.

articles.headline: DRDO Successfully Test-Fires Indigenous 'Agni-P' New Generation Ballistic Missile
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) has successfully conducted the flight test of the Agni-P (Prime) new generation ballistic missile.
• Agni-P is a canistered, solid-propellant ballistic missile with advanced maneuverability and a range of 1,000 to 2,000 kilometers.
• The test validated the missile's state-of-the-art propulsion system, guidance, and control mechanisms.
• This successful trial significantly enhances India's strategic deterrent capabilities and strengthens its 'Make in India' initiative in defence.
• The Agni-P is designed to be lighter and more agile than previous Agni series missiles, offering greater operational flexibility.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Security challenges and their management in border areas; Defence technology
prelimsPoints: Agni-P is a new generation variant of the Agni series missiles.
It is a medium-range ballistic missile (MRBM).
Developed by DRDO, it is a canistered missile (can be launched from road/rail).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous missile development like Agni-P for India's strategic autonomy and national security.
Analyze how advancements in missile technology contribute to India's credible minimum deterrence posture.
Examine the role of DRDO in enhancing India's defence capabilities and promoting self-reliance in the defence sector.
articles.imageDescription: A sleek Agni-P missile being launched from a mobile launcher.
articles.trivia: The Agni series of missiles forms the backbone of India's nuclear weapons delivery systems.

articles.headline: Digital India Initiative Pushes for Accelerated Rural Broadband Expansion
articles.explainer:
• The government's Digital India initiative is intensifying efforts to bridge the digital divide by accelerating rural broadband connectivity.
• New targets have been set for the BharatNet project, aiming to connect all gram panchayats with high-speed internet by the revised deadline.
• The focus is on leveraging various technologies, including optical fiber and satellite broadband, to ensure last-mile connectivity.
• This expansion is crucial for promoting digital literacy, e-governance services, online education, and digital payments in rural areas.
• Public-private partnerships are being encouraged to expedite infrastructure deployment and ensure sustainable service delivery.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: BharatNet project aims to provide broadband connectivity to all Gram Panchayats.
Digital India is a flagship program launched in 2015.
Focus on 'Digital infrastructure as a utility to every citizen'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic benefits of universal rural broadband connectivity for India's inclusive growth.
Analyze the challenges faced by the BharatNet project and suggest measures for its effective and timely implementation.
Examine how enhanced digital infrastructure can transform rural economies and empower local communities.
articles.imageDescription: A network of optical fiber cables extending towards a rural village, symbolizing connectivity.
articles.trivia: India has the world's second-largest internet user base, but rural penetration still lags significantly.

articles.headline: Monsoon Progresses Across India, IMD Issues Advisory for Farmers and Disaster Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• The Southwest Monsoon has progressed steadily across various parts of India, bringing much-needed rainfall to agricultural regions.
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued advisories for farmers, recommending appropriate cropping patterns and water management practices.
• Forecasts indicate normal to above-normal rainfall for most regions, crucial for the upcoming Kharif season.
• States prone to floods and landslides have been urged to enhance disaster preparedness, with NDRF teams on standby.
• The IMD is continuously monitoring monsoon dynamics, including potential low-pressure systems, to provide timely updates.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Southwest Monsoon accounts for over 70% of India's annual rainfall.
Kharif crops are sown with the onset of monsoon.
IMD is the primary agency for weather forecasting in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic significance of the monsoon for India's agriculture and overall economy.
Analyze the challenges posed by erratic monsoon patterns and extreme weather events, and measures to enhance climate resilience in agriculture.
Examine the role of early warning systems and disaster management strategies in mitigating monsoon-related risks.
articles.imageDescription: Rain-soaked fields with farmers preparing for sowing, under a cloudy sky.
articles.trivia: The word 'monsoon' is derived from the Arabic word 'mausim', meaning 'season'.

articles.headline: India Pushes for Equitable Reforms at WTO Ministerial Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India actively participated in the World Trade Organization (WTO) Ministerial Meeting, advocating for comprehensive reforms to ensure equity and fairness in global trade.
• Key areas of focus for India included special and differential treatment for developing countries, public stockholding for food security, and resolution of the Appellate Body impasse.
• India reiterated its commitment to a multilateral trading system but emphasized the need for its rules to reflect the evolving global economic landscape.
• Discussions also touched upon e-commerce regulations and fisheries subsidies, where India sought balanced outcomes.
• The meeting highlighted the ongoing challenges in achieving consensus among diverse member interests within the WTO.
gsSubTopics: Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: WTO is the only global international organization dealing with the rules of trade between nations.
India is a founding member of WTO (formerly GATT).
Public stockholding for food security is a major demand of developing countries at WTO.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's stance on WTO reforms and its implications for developing countries and global trade rules.
Discuss the challenges in achieving consensus on critical issues like public stockholding and fisheries subsidies within the WTO framework.
Examine how the weakening of the WTO's dispute settlement mechanism impacts multilateral trade and India's trade interests.
articles.imageDescription: The WTO headquarters building in Geneva, Switzerland.
articles.trivia: The WTO's highest decision-making body is the Ministerial Conference, which usually meets every two years.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Enhanced Transparency for Electoral Bonds and Political Funding
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee has submitted a report recommending significant reforms to the electoral bond scheme and overall political funding.
• The panel advocated for greater transparency, including mandatory disclosure of donors' identities and the recipient political parties.
• It suggested exploring alternative mechanisms for transparent political funding that balance donor privacy with public accountability.
• The recommendations aim to address concerns raised about the opaqueness of electoral bonds and their potential impact on democratic processes.
• The report calls for a comprehensive review of the Representation of the People Act to incorporate these reforms.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds were introduced in 2018.
They are interest-free bearer instruments.
The Supreme Court had struck down the Electoral Bond scheme in 2024.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the electoral bond scheme in terms of transparency and democratic accountability.
Analyze the recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee and their potential impact on political funding in India.
Suggest comprehensive electoral reforms that can ensure greater transparency and fairness in political financing.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing political funding and elections.
articles.trivia: Before electoral bonds, political parties primarily relied on cash donations and corporate contributions.

articles.headline: Government Intensifies Millets Promotion for Nutritional Security and Farmers' Income
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare has launched new initiatives to significantly boost the production and consumption of millets.
• These efforts align with the 'International Year of Millets' and India's focus on promoting 'nutri-cereals' for food security.
• The plan includes providing incentives to farmers for millet cultivation, improving processing infrastructure, and creating market linkages.
• Millets are recognized for their high nutritional value, climate resilience, and lower water footprint compared to staple crops like rice and wheat.
• The government aims to enhance farmers' income by diversifying cropping patterns and tapping into growing domestic and international demand for millets.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
Food processing and related industries in India- scope and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing
prelimsPoints: 2023 was declared as the 'International Year of Millets' by the UN, at India's proposal.
Major millets include Jowar, Bajra, Ragi.
Millets are drought-resistant and have high nutritional content (iron, calcium, fiber).
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted benefits of promoting millets for India's nutritional security, agricultural sustainability, and farmers' welfare.
Analyze the challenges in increasing millet production and consumption, and suggest policy interventions to overcome them.
Examine how millets can contribute to climate-resilient agriculture and address the changing dietary needs of the population.
articles.imageDescription: Various types of millets displayed in bowls, with a farmer in the background.
articles.trivia: India is the largest producer of millets in the world.

articles.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Renewable Energy Grid Integration and Storage Solutions
articles.explainer:
• India is hosting a major international conference focused on advanced solutions for renewable energy grid integration and energy storage.
• The conference brings together global experts, policymakers, and industry leaders to discuss challenges and innovations in scaling up green energy.
• Key topics include smart grid technologies, battery storage, green hydrogen, and policy frameworks for a robust renewable energy ecosystem.
• India showcased its progress in renewable energy deployment and its ambitious targets for achieving net-zero emissions.
• The event aims to foster international collaboration and technology transfer to accelerate the global energy transition.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: India has ambitious targets for renewable energy capacity addition.
Grid integration and storage are critical challenges for high renewable energy penetration.
Green Hydrogen is seen as a key fuel for decarbonization.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities in integrating a high share of renewable energy into India's national grid.
Analyze the role of advanced energy storage solutions and green hydrogen in achieving India's clean energy goals.
Examine how international collaboration and technology transfer can accelerate India's transition to a sustainable energy future.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels and wind turbines connected to a smart grid, with a battery storage facility.
articles.trivia: India is the world's third-largest energy consumer.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician Sanjay Raj Subba Passes Away at 65
articles.explainer:
• Sanjay Raj Subba, a prominent politician, passed away today at the age of 65.
• Subba had a long and impactful career, serving multiple terms as a legislator and holding various ministerial portfolios.
• He was known for his advocacy on issues related to regional development and social justice.
• His demise marks the end of an era for his political party and constituency.
• Tributes poured in from political leaders across the spectrum, acknowledging his contributions to public life.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
articles.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of a dignified politician.
articles.trivia: Obituaries of prominent public figures often highlight their contributions to policy-making and social change.

articles.headline: National Quantum Mission Receives Enhanced Funding for Research and Development
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology has announced a significant increase in funding for the National Quantum Mission (NQM).
• The enhanced budget aims to accelerate research and development in quantum technologies, including quantum computing, communication, and sensing.
• India seeks to position itself as a global leader in quantum science, fostering indigenous capabilities and talent.
• The mission involves collaborations between academic institutions, research labs, and private industry to drive innovation.
• This push is critical for future strategic applications in defense, finance, healthcare, and secure communication.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: National Quantum Mission (NQM) was launched in 2023.
It aims to make India a leader in quantum technologies.
Quantum computing uses 'qubits' which can exist in multiple states simultaneously.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential transformative impact of quantum technologies on various sectors, including defense, finance, and healthcare.
Analyze the challenges India faces in developing a robust quantum ecosystem and how the NQM addresses these.
Examine the ethical and security implications of advancements in quantum computing and communication.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of quantum particles and connections, symbolizing quantum technology.
articles.trivia: Quantum entanglement, a key concept in quantum mechanics, was famously described by Einstein as 'spooky action at a distance'.

articles.headline: India Champions Equitable Global Health Governance and Vaccine Access
articles.explainer:
• India continued its proactive role in global health forums, advocating for equitable access to vaccines, medicines, and healthcare technologies.
• At a recent international health summit, India emphasized strengthening multilateral health institutions and reforming global health governance structures.
• The nation highlighted its contributions as a 'pharmacy of the world' and its commitment to public health initiatives, especially for the Global South.
• Discussions included preparedness for future pandemics, antimicrobial resistance, and achieving universal health coverage.
• India called for greater solidarity and resource mobilization to address health disparities worldwide.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: India is a major global producer of vaccines and generic medicines.
WHO is the specialized agency of the UN responsible for international public health.
Global South refers to developing countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role and influence in shaping global health policies and initiatives.
Discuss the challenges in achieving equitable global health governance and how India's stance contributes to addressing them.
Examine the concept of 'vaccine nationalism' and India's efforts to promote 'vaccine internationalism' during global health crises.
articles.imageDescription: A globe surrounded by medical symbols, representing global health cooperation.
articles.trivia: India's 'Jan Aushadhi Kendras' aim to provide quality generic medicines at affordable prices.

articles.headline: Government Provides Update on Implementation of Digital Personal Data Protection Act
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released an update on the ongoing implementation of the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• The update outlines the progress in establishing the Data Protection Board of India and framing detailed rules and regulations.
• It emphasizes the importance of balancing individual privacy rights with the need for data innovation and economic growth.
• Consultations are ongoing with various stakeholders, including industry bodies, legal experts, and civil society organizations.
• The government aims for a phased and smooth transition to the new data protection regime, ensuring compliance from all entities handling personal data.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Information and Communication Technology (ICT)
Cyber Security
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Act, 2023, is India's primary law for personal data protection.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
It defines 'Data Principal' (individual) and 'Data Fiduciary' (entity processing data).
mainsPoints: Discuss the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its significance for individual privacy in the digital age.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a diverse and digitally evolving country like India.
Examine the balance struck by the DPDP Act between individual privacy rights, state interests, and the promotion of data-driven innovation.
articles.imageDescription: A padlock on a digital screen, symbolizing data security and privacy.
articles.trivia: The right to privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court of India in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).

highlights: India and Canada announce restoration of normal diplomatic relations, easing tensions from the 2023 dispute.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains the repo rate, prioritizing inflation control amidst global uncertainties.
Supreme Court declares 'Right to Clean Air' as a fundamental right under Article 21, boosting environmental governance.
ISRO unveils plans for 'Chandrayaan-4' for advanced lunar polar exploration, building on previous successes.
Government launches PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana 5.0, focusing on future-ready skills and inclusive youth empowerment.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent agreement between India and Canada to restore normal diplomatic relations, consider the following statements:
1. The dispute primarily began over Canadian allegations regarding India's involvement in the killing of Sikh separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar.
2. The agreement involves immediate resumption of visa services for all categories between the two countries.
3. The resolution signifies a mutual desire to enhance cooperation in security and trade.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The dispute centered on allegations regarding the killing of Hardeep Singh Nijjar. Statement 3 is correct. The restoration of ties aims to rebuild trust and cooperation. Statement 2 is likely incorrect as 'immediate resumption of visa services for all categories' might be a gradual process, not necessarily an immediate outcome of the diplomatic agreement to normalize relations. The prompt says 'resumption of full bilateral engagements' which implies a broader scope than just visa services and might take time.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India:
1. It is a statutory body constituted under the RBI Act, 1934.
2. The Governor of RBI is the ex-officio Chairperson of the MPC.
3. The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The MPC was constituted under Section 45ZB of the amended RBI Act, 1934 (Statement 1). The RBI Governor is indeed its ex-officio Chairperson (Statement 2). Its mandated objective is to maintain price stability, while also considering growth (Statement 3).

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to the Supreme Court's declaration of 'Right to Clean Air' as a fundamental right, which Article of the Indian Constitution is primarily invoked?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court has consistently interpreted the 'Right to Life' under Article 21 of the Constitution to include various facets of dignified living, such as the right to clean environment, clean water, and now, clean air.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about ISRO's Chandrayaan missions is correct?
(a) Chandrayaan-1 was the first mission to successfully soft-land a rover on the lunar surface.
(b) Chandrayaan-2 successfully placed an orbiter around the Moon, but its lander-rover mission failed.
(c) Chandrayaan-3 confirmed the presence of water molecules on the Moon's surface.
(d) Chandrayaan-4 aims to be India's first mission to explore the lunar north pole.
options: (a) Chandrayaan-1 was the first mission to successfully soft-land a rover on the lunar surface.
(b) Chandrayaan-2 successfully placed an orbiter around the Moon, but its lander-rover mission failed.
(c) Chandrayaan-3 confirmed the presence of water molecules on the Moon's surface.
(d) Chandrayaan-4 aims to be India's first mission to explore the lunar north pole.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Chandrayaan-2 successfully placed an orbiter around the Moon, but its lander-rover mission failed.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 confirmed water molecules. Chandrayaan-2's lander (Vikram) and rover (Pragyan) failed to soft-land, but the orbiter is still functional. Chandrayaan-3 successfully soft-landed a rover. Chandrayaan-4 is for 'lunar polar exploration' (south pole is the primary interest for water ice, not necessarily north pole exclusively).

prelimsMocks.question: The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is a flagship scheme of which Union Ministry?
options: (d) Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsMocks.explanation: PMKVY is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) implemented through the National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC).

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the trajectory of India-Canada relations in recent years, particularly in light of the diplomatic dispute over the Nijjar killing and its subsequent resolution. What are the geopolitical implications of their renewed cooperation for India's strategic interests?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the India-Canada relationship and the specific dispute. Detail the reasons for the breakdown and the steps towards resolution. Discuss the impact of the strained ties on trade, diaspora, and strategic partnerships. Analyze the geopolitical implications of renewed cooperation, especially in the context of the Indo-Pacific and balancing Western alliances. Conclude with the way forward for strengthening the relationship.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's pronouncement of the 'Right to Clean Air' as a fundamental right marks a significant step towards environmental justice. Discuss the implications of this ruling for environmental governance and public health in India, and suggest measures for its effective implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—significant provisions; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by explaining the significance of the SC's ruling and its basis in Article 21. Elucidate the implications for environmental governance (increased accountability, policy mandates, judicial oversight) and public health (focus on pollution control, health infrastructure). Identify challenges in implementation (inter-state cooperation, industrial compliance, funding). Suggest multi-pronged measures for effective implementation, including legal, policy, technological, and public participation aspects.

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana in addressing India's skill gap and harnessing its demographic dividend. What reforms are necessary to make skill development truly inclusive and industry-relevant?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections; Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Human Resources; GS3: Inclusive Growth.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's demographic dividend and the challenge of skill gap. Describe PMKVY's objectives and previous phases, assessing its successes (e.g., reach, awareness) and limitations (e.g., quality, placement rates, relevance). Discuss how PMKVY 5.0 aims to address these. Suggest comprehensive reforms focusing on industry-academia linkage, demand-driven training, quality assurance, entrepreneurship promotion, digital skilling, and ensuring inclusivity for marginalized groups. Conclude with the importance of skill development for economic growth and social equity.

quickBytes.text: The dispute between India and Canada over the killing of Hardeep Singh Nijjar began in 2023.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the 'Right to Life and Personal Liberty'.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI has six members.
India's first lunar mission, Chandrayaan-1, was launched in 2008.
The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
The Quad grouping comprises India, the United States, Japan, and Australia.
The Agni-P (Prime) is a new generation ballistic missile developed by DRDO.
BharatNet project aims to provide broadband connectivity to all Gram Panchayats.
2023 was designated as the 'International Year of Millets' by the United Nations.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, establishes the Data Protection Board of India.

GS1.headline: Monsoon Progresses Across India, IMD Issues Advisory for Farmers and Disaster Preparedness
GS1.explainer:
• The Southwest Monsoon has progressed steadily across various parts of India, bringing much-needed rainfall to agricultural regions.
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued advisories for farmers, recommending appropriate cropping patterns and water management practices.
• Forecasts indicate normal to above-normal rainfall for most regions, crucial for the upcoming Kharif season.
• States prone to floods and landslides have been urged to enhance disaster preparedness, with NDRF teams on standby.
• The IMD is continuously monitoring monsoon dynamics, including potential low-pressure systems, to provide timely updates.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Southwest Monsoon accounts for over 70% of India's annual rainfall.
Kharif crops are sown with the onset of monsoon.
IMD is the primary agency for weather forecasting in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic significance of the monsoon for India's agriculture and overall economy.
Analyze the challenges posed by erratic monsoon patterns and extreme weather events, and measures to enhance climate resilience in agriculture.
Examine the role of early warning systems and disaster management strategies in mitigating monsoon-related risks.
GS1.imageDescription: Rain-soaked fields with farmers preparing for sowing, under a cloudy sky.
GS1.trivia: The word 'monsoon' is derived from the Arabic word 'mausim', meaning 'season'.

GS2.headline: India and Canada Agree to Restore Normal Diplomatic Relations Post-Dispute
GS2.explainer:
• India and Canada have announced a significant agreement to normalize diplomatic ties, marking a de-escalation of tensions that began in 2023.
• The dispute originated from Canadian allegations regarding India's potential involvement in the killing of Sikh separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar in Vancouver.
• The agreement is expected to facilitate the return of high commissioners and other diplomatic staff, along with a resumption of full bilateral engagements.
• This move signals a mutual desire to rebuild trust and cooperation on various fronts, including trade, security, and people-to-people exchanges.
• Analysts suggest the resolution reflects diplomatic efforts from both sides, possibly influenced by pressure from international partners to stabilize relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Dispute originated in 2023 over Hardeep Singh Nijjar's killing.
Nijjar was a prominent leader in the Khalistan movement.
Agreement aims to restore 'normal diplomatic relations'.
mainsPoints: Impact of the dispute on India's global image and diaspora relations.
Geopolitical implications of renewed cooperation between India and Canada.
Challenges and opportunities in rebuilding trust and expanding bilateral trade.
Role of external actors/allies in mediating or influencing the resolution.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Canada side-by-side, symbolizing renewed diplomatic ties.
GS2.trivia: Canada is home to one of the largest Sikh diasporas outside of Punjab, India, which often influences bilateral dynamics.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Declares Right to Clean Air as Fundamental Right Under Article 21
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has delivered a landmark judgment, affirming that the 'Right to Clean Air' is an integral part of the fundamental 'Right to Life' guaranteed by Article 21 of the Constitution.
• This ruling comes in response to a petition highlighting severe air pollution levels across various Indian cities, impacting public health.
• The Court emphasized the state's obligation to protect and improve the environment, ensuring citizens' right to live with dignity.
• The judgment is expected to bolster environmental protection laws and push for more stringent measures against pollution.
• It calls upon central and state governments to formulate and implement comprehensive strategies to combat air pollution effectively.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Right to Life is enshrined in Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
The judgment links environmental quality directly to fundamental rights.
Various environmental rights have been read into Article 21 by the SC over time (e.g., clean water, healthy environment).
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of this judgment for environmental governance and policy-making in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the 'Right to Clean Air' across diverse regions and industrial landscapes.
Examine the role of judicial intervention in strengthening environmental protection and public health in India.
Suggest a multi-pronged strategy for governments to effectively uphold this fundamental right.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and an Indian Constitution book, symbolizing a landmark legal decision.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to a Healthy Environment' is recognized in many international human rights instruments.

GS2.headline: Government Launches PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana 5.0, Focus on Future-Ready Skills
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship has rolled out the fifth phase of the Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY 5.0).
• This iteration places a strong emphasis on training youth in emerging technologies and industry-relevant skills like AI, IoT, data science, and sustainable practices.
• PMKVY 5.0 aims to align skill development with the demands of the rapidly evolving job market and promote entrepreneurship.
• The scheme incorporates enhanced digital learning modules, industry apprenticeships, and outcome-based assessments.
• Special provisions are included to ensure inclusive participation from marginalized communities and rural areas, bridging the skill gap across India.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Inclusive Growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: PMKVY is a flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
It aims to provide skill training to Indian youth.
PMKVY 5.0 focuses on 'future skills' and 'demand-driven' training.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the success and limitations of previous PMKVY phases in addressing India's skill gap.
Discuss how PMKVY 5.0's focus on emerging technologies can contribute to India's economic growth and global competitiveness.
Suggest measures to ensure that skill development programs are inclusive, industry-relevant, and lead to sustainable employment opportunities.
GS2.imageDescription: Young people engaged in various vocational training activities, symbolizing skill development.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, making skill development crucial for harnessing its demographic dividend.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Global South Concerns at Quad Leaders' Summit
GS2.explainer:
• India played a pivotal role in advocating for the interests and concerns of the Global South at the recently concluded Quad Leaders' Summit.
• Prime Minister highlighted issues such as climate justice, equitable access to technology, and sustainable development for developing nations.
• The Quad leaders discussed regional security, maritime cooperation, critical and emerging technologies, and infrastructure development.
• While maintaining its focus on a free and open Indo-Pacific, India emphasized the need for inclusive growth and addressing global challenges with a broader perspective.
• The summit reaffirmed the commitment of Quad nations (India, USA, Japan, Australia) to a rules-based international order and regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Quad members are India, USA, Japan, and Australia.
Quad focuses on a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
India often acts as a voice for the Global South in international forums.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic rationale for engaging with the Quad while simultaneously championing the Global South.
Discuss the effectiveness of the Quad in addressing both traditional security concerns and contemporary challenges like climate change and technology access.
Examine the potential benefits and drawbacks of India's multi-alignment foreign policy approach in the context of the Quad.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders of the Quad nations shaking hands, with flags in the background.
GS2.trivia: The Quad was initially conceived in 2007 but gained significant momentum and formalization in the late 2010s.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Enhanced Transparency for Electoral Bonds and Political Funding
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee has submitted a report recommending significant reforms to the electoral bond scheme and overall political funding.
• The panel advocated for greater transparency, including mandatory disclosure of donors' identities and the recipient political parties.
• It suggested exploring alternative mechanisms for transparent political funding that balance donor privacy with public accountability.
• The recommendations aim to address concerns raised about the opaqueness of electoral bonds and their potential impact on democratic processes.
• The report calls for a comprehensive review of the Representation of the People Act to incorporate these reforms.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds were introduced in 2018.
They are interest-free bearer instruments.
The Supreme Court had struck down the Electoral Bond scheme in 2024.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the electoral bond scheme in terms of transparency and democratic accountability.
Analyze the recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee and their potential impact on political funding in India.
Suggest comprehensive electoral reforms that can ensure greater transparency and fairness in political financing.
GS2.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing political funding and elections.
GS2.trivia: Before electoral bonds, political parties primarily relied on cash donations and corporate contributions.

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician Sanjay Raj Subba Passes Away at 65
GS2.explainer:
• Sanjay Raj Subba, a prominent politician, passed away today at the age of 65.
• Subba had a long and impactful career, serving multiple terms as a legislator and holding various ministerial portfolios.
• He was known for his advocacy on issues related to regional development and social justice.
• His demise marks the end of an era for his political party and constituency.
• Tributes poured in from political leaders across the spectrum, acknowledging his contributions to public life.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
GS2.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of a dignified politician.
GS2.trivia: Obituaries of prominent public figures often highlight their contributions to policy-making and social change.

GS2.headline: India Champions Equitable Global Health Governance and Vaccine Access
GS2.explainer:
• India continued its proactive role in global health forums, advocating for equitable access to vaccines, medicines, and healthcare technologies.
• At a recent international health summit, India emphasized strengthening multilateral health institutions and reforming global health governance structures.
• The nation highlighted its contributions as a 'pharmacy of the world' and its commitment to public health initiatives, especially for the Global South.
• Discussions included preparedness for future pandemics, antimicrobial resistance, and achieving universal health coverage.
• India called for greater solidarity and resource mobilization to address health disparities worldwide.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: India is a major global producer of vaccines and generic medicines.
WHO is the specialized agency of the UN responsible for international public health.
Global South refers to developing countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role and influence in shaping global health policies and initiatives.
Discuss the challenges in achieving equitable global health governance and how India's stance contributes to addressing them.
Examine the concept of 'vaccine nationalism' and India's efforts to promote 'vaccine internationalism' during global health crises.
GS2.imageDescription: A globe surrounded by medical symbols, representing global health cooperation.
GS2.trivia: India's 'Jan Aushadhi Kendras' aim to provide quality generic medicines at affordable prices.

GS2.headline: Government Provides Update on Implementation of Digital Personal Data Protection Act
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released an update on the ongoing implementation of the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• The update outlines the progress in establishing the Data Protection Board of India and framing detailed rules and regulations.
• It emphasizes the importance of balancing individual privacy rights with the need for data innovation and economic growth.
• Consultations are ongoing with various stakeholders, including industry bodies, legal experts, and civil society organizations.
• The government aims for a phased and smooth transition to the new data protection regime, ensuring compliance from all entities handling personal data.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Information and Communication Technology (ICT)
Cyber Security
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Act, 2023, is India's primary law for personal data protection.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
It defines 'Data Principal' (individual) and 'Data Fiduciary' (entity processing data).
mainsPoints: Discuss the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its significance for individual privacy in the digital age.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a diverse and digitally evolving country like India.
Examine the balance struck by the DPDP Act between individual privacy rights, state interests, and the promotion of data-driven innovation.
GS2.imageDescription: A padlock on a digital screen, symbolizing data security and privacy.
GS2.trivia: The right to privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court of India in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).

GS3.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Watch
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision comes as the MPC continues to prioritize inflation control while supporting economic growth.
• The central bank noted persistent global uncertainties and domestic food inflation as key factors influencing its cautious stance.
• Liquidity conditions in the banking system were also reviewed, with the RBI reiterating its commitment to calibrate policy as needed.
• Experts anticipate that the RBI will maintain a vigilant approach, with any rate cuts contingent on sustained moderation in inflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC consists of 6 members (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by Government).
Primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in managing domestic and global economic headwinds.
Examine the potential impact of a prolonged status quo on interest rates on various sectors of the Indian economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was constituted under Section 45ZB of the amended RBI Act, 1934, in 2016.

GS3.headline: ISRO Unveils Ambitious Plans for 'Chandrayaan-4' Lunar Polar Exploration Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has announced its next major lunar mission, 'Chandrayaan-4', focusing on advanced polar exploration.
• The mission aims to conduct in-depth studies of the Moon's polar regions, particularly searching for water ice and understanding lunar geology.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a more complex mission, potentially involving a larger lander, rover, and even a sample return capability.
• This mission builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-1 and Chandrayaan-3, further solidifying India's position in lunar science.
• International collaborations are also being explored for components or scientific instruments, enhancing global space partnerships.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-1 confirmed presence of water molecules on Moon.
Chandrayaan-3 successfully soft-landed near the lunar south pole.
Chandrayaan-4 aims for more advanced studies, potentially including sample return.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar polar regions for future space exploration and potential resource utilization.
Analyze how ISRO's lunar missions contribute to India's scientific capabilities and global standing in space technology.
Examine the economic and strategic benefits of investing in advanced space missions like Chandrayaan-4.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of Chandrayaan-4 spacecraft orbiting the Moon, focusing on the polar region.
GS3.trivia: The Moon's polar regions are of particular interest due to permanently shadowed craters believed to harbor significant amounts of water ice.

GS3.headline: New National Tiger Conservation Plan Prioritizes Corridor Protection and Community Involvement
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has unveiled an updated National Tiger Conservation Plan (NTCP).
• The revised plan places a strong emphasis on securing and restoring critical tiger corridors to ensure genetic exchange and prevent habitat fragmentation.
• It integrates local communities as key stakeholders in conservation efforts, promoting sustainable livelihoods and reducing human-wildlife conflict.
• Advanced surveillance technologies, including drones and AI-powered monitoring, will be deployed for more effective anti-poaching measures.
• The plan also aims to strengthen transboundary cooperation with neighboring countries for better management of shared tiger habitats.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Environmental governance
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
NTCA (National Tiger Conservation Authority) is a statutory body.
Tiger corridors are crucial for genetic flow between fragmented tiger populations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in tiger conservation in India and how the new NTCP addresses them.
Analyze the importance of community participation and transboundary cooperation in successful wildlife conservation.
Examine the role of technology in anti-poaching and habitat monitoring efforts within the new plan.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage, symbolizing conservation efforts.
GS3.trivia: India is home to approximately 70% of the world's wild tiger population.

GS3.headline: DRDO Successfully Test-Fires Indigenous 'Agni-P' New Generation Ballistic Missile
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) has successfully conducted the flight test of the Agni-P (Prime) new generation ballistic missile.
• Agni-P is a canistered, solid-propellant ballistic missile with advanced maneuverability and a range of 1,000 to 2,000 kilometers.
• The test validated the missile's state-of-the-art propulsion system, guidance, and control mechanisms.
• This successful trial significantly enhances India's strategic deterrent capabilities and strengthens its 'Make in India' initiative in defence.
• The Agni-P is designed to be lighter and more agile than previous Agni series missiles, offering greater operational flexibility.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Security challenges and their management in border areas; Defence technology
prelimsPoints: Agni-P is a new generation variant of the Agni series missiles.
It is a medium-range ballistic missile (MRBM).
Developed by DRDO, it is a canistered missile (can be launched from road/rail).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous missile development like Agni-P for India's strategic autonomy and national security.
Analyze how advancements in missile technology contribute to India's credible minimum deterrence posture.
Examine the role of DRDO in enhancing India's defence capabilities and promoting self-reliance in the defence sector.
GS3.imageDescription: A sleek Agni-P missile being launched from a mobile launcher.
GS3.trivia: The Agni series of missiles forms the backbone of India's nuclear weapons delivery systems.

GS3.headline: Digital India Initiative Pushes for Accelerated Rural Broadband Expansion
GS3.explainer:
• The government's Digital India initiative is intensifying efforts to bridge the digital divide by accelerating rural broadband connectivity.
• New targets have been set for the BharatNet project, aiming to connect all gram panchayats with high-speed internet by the revised deadline.
• The focus is on leveraging various technologies, including optical fiber and satellite broadband, to ensure last-mile connectivity.
• This expansion is crucial for promoting digital literacy, e-governance services, online education, and digital payments in rural areas.
• Public-private partnerships are being encouraged to expedite infrastructure deployment and ensure sustainable service delivery.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: BharatNet project aims to provide broadband connectivity to all Gram Panchayats.
Digital India is a flagship program launched in 2015.
Focus on 'Digital infrastructure as a utility to every citizen'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic benefits of universal rural broadband connectivity for India's inclusive growth.
Analyze the challenges faced by the BharatNet project and suggest measures for its effective and timely implementation.
Examine how enhanced digital infrastructure can transform rural economies and empower local communities.
GS3.imageDescription: A network of optical fiber cables extending towards a rural village, symbolizing connectivity.
GS3.trivia: India has the world's second-largest internet user base, but rural penetration still lags significantly.

GS3.headline: India Pushes for Equitable Reforms at WTO Ministerial Meeting
GS3.explainer:
• India actively participated in the World Trade Organization (WTO) Ministerial Meeting, advocating for comprehensive reforms to ensure equity and fairness in global trade.
• Key areas of focus for India included special and differential treatment for developing countries, public stockholding for food security, and resolution of the Appellate Body impasse.
• India reiterated its commitment to a multilateral trading system but emphasized the need for its rules to reflect the evolving global economic landscape.
• Discussions also touched upon e-commerce regulations and fisheries subsidies, where India sought balanced outcomes.
• The meeting highlighted the ongoing challenges in achieving consensus among diverse member interests within the WTO.
gsSubTopics: Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: WTO is the only global international organization dealing with the rules of trade between nations.
India is a founding member of WTO (formerly GATT).
Public stockholding for food security is a major demand of developing countries at WTO.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's stance on WTO reforms and its implications for developing countries and global trade rules.
Discuss the challenges in achieving consensus on critical issues like public stockholding and fisheries subsidies within the WTO framework.
Examine how the weakening of the WTO's dispute settlement mechanism impacts multilateral trade and India's trade interests.
GS3.imageDescription: The WTO headquarters building in Geneva, Switzerland.
GS3.trivia: The WTO's highest decision-making body is the Ministerial Conference, which usually meets every two years.

GS3.headline: Government Intensifies Millets Promotion for Nutritional Security and Farmers' Income
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare has launched new initiatives to significantly boost the production and consumption of millets.
• These efforts align with the 'International Year of Millets' and India's focus on promoting 'nutri-cereals' for food security.
• The plan includes providing incentives to farmers for millet cultivation, improving processing infrastructure, and creating market linkages.
• Millets are recognized for their high nutritional value, climate resilience, and lower water footprint compared to staple crops like rice and wheat.
• The government aims to enhance farmers' income by diversifying cropping patterns and tapping into growing domestic and international demand for millets.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
Food processing and related industries in India- scope and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing
prelimsPoints: 2023 was declared as the 'International Year of Millets' by the UN, at India's proposal.
Major millets include Jowar, Bajra, Ragi.
Millets are drought-resistant and have high nutritional content (iron, calcium, fiber).
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted benefits of promoting millets for India's nutritional security, agricultural sustainability, and farmers' welfare.
Analyze the challenges in increasing millet production and consumption, and suggest policy interventions to overcome them.
Examine how millets can contribute to climate-resilient agriculture and address the changing dietary needs of the population.
GS3.imageDescription: Various types of millets displayed in bowls, with a farmer in the background.
GS3.trivia: India is the largest producer of millets in the world.

GS3.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Renewable Energy Grid Integration and Storage Solutions
GS3.explainer:
• India is hosting a major international conference focused on advanced solutions for renewable energy grid integration and energy storage.
• The conference brings together global experts, policymakers, and industry leaders to discuss challenges and innovations in scaling up green energy.
• Key topics include smart grid technologies, battery storage, green hydrogen, and policy frameworks for a robust renewable energy ecosystem.
• India showcased its progress in renewable energy deployment and its ambitious targets for achieving net-zero emissions.
• The event aims to foster international collaboration and technology transfer to accelerate the global energy transition.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: India has ambitious targets for renewable energy capacity addition.
Grid integration and storage are critical challenges for high renewable energy penetration.
Green Hydrogen is seen as a key fuel for decarbonization.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities in integrating a high share of renewable energy into India's national grid.
Analyze the role of advanced energy storage solutions and green hydrogen in achieving India's clean energy goals.
Examine how international collaboration and technology transfer can accelerate India's transition to a sustainable energy future.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels and wind turbines connected to a smart grid, with a battery storage facility.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's third-largest energy consumer.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission Receives Enhanced Funding for Research and Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology has announced a significant increase in funding for the National Quantum Mission (NQM).
• The enhanced budget aims to accelerate research and development in quantum technologies, including quantum computing, communication, and sensing.
• India seeks to position itself as a global leader in quantum science, fostering indigenous capabilities and talent.
• The mission involves collaborations between academic institutions, research labs, and private industry to drive innovation.
• This push is critical for future strategic applications in defense, finance, healthcare, and secure communication.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: National Quantum Mission (NQM) was launched in 2023.
It aims to make India a leader in quantum technologies.
Quantum computing uses 'qubits' which can exist in multiple states simultaneously.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential transformative impact of quantum technologies on various sectors, including defense, finance, and healthcare.
Analyze the challenges India faces in developing a robust quantum ecosystem and how the NQM addresses these.
Examine the ethical and security implications of advancements in quantum computing and communication.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract representation of quantum particles and connections, symbolizing quantum technology.
GS3.trivia: Quantum entanglement, a key concept in quantum mechanics, was famously described by Einstein as 'spooky action at a distance'.
